description: Cohesion and coherence signatures for suspicious pseudo-profound prose.

signatures.title: Jargon spray with weak local cohesion
signatures.description: The document introduces many unique concept words while failing to reuse or bridge them across adjacent sentences.

rules.why: Most nouns are one-off terms, which suggests topic churn rather than progressive explanation.
rules.fix: Reuse 3-5 anchor nouns across neighboring sentences so each paragraph develops stable concepts.

rules.why: Content vocabulary is highly novel sentence to sentence, often seen in polished-sounding but thin text.
rules.fix: Consolidate terminology and remove near-synonymous buzzwords that do not add new meaning.

rules.why: Adjacent sentences rarely share noun concepts, indicating poor local chaining.
rules.fix: Add explicit cross-sentence bridges by repeating key entities and referring back to prior claims.

rules.why: Core content terms are not repeated enough to form a coherent thread.
rules.fix: Revisit each section and deliberately restate central terms when introducing implications or constraints.

rules.why: Even synonym-level noun continuity is low, reinforcing the sparse cohesion signal.
rules.fix: Keep semantic neighborhoods tight: define canonical terms and avoid constant conceptual relabeling.

signatures.title: Floating claims with weak logical linkage
signatures.description: Claims appear semantically disconnected and under-signaled by logical or causal connectives.

rules.why: Low connective density often indicates isolated assertions instead of reasoned progression.
rules.fix: Introduce explicit causal and contrastive relations (because, therefore, however, for example).

rules.why: Adjacent sentences have low semantic continuity in vector space.
rules.fix: Rewrite transitions so each sentence explicitly builds on the previous sentence's meaning.

rules.why: Arguments (noun/pronoun carriers) are not sustained across sentence boundaries.
rules.fix: Carry forward actors and objects across 2-3 sentences before introducing new entities.

signatures.title: Semantic veneer without lexical thread
signatures.description: The text can look semantically smooth in embeddings while lacking concrete lexical continuity.

rules.why: Global semantic vectors appear coherent.
rules.fix: Pair high-level abstraction with concrete repeated entities, constraints, and examples.

rules.why: Noun inventory is too novel for stable explanatory prose.
rules.fix: Reduce conceptual churn by defining a glossary and sticking to canonical nouns.

rules.why: Local noun overlap is too weak for coherent paragraph flow.
rules.fix: Use sentence openers that reference the previous sentence's main entity.

rules.why: Low recurrence of content lemmas signals weak thematic anchoring.
rules.fix: Reinforce recurring claims with repeated terminology and explicit definitions.

signatures.title: Logic gap with weak entity carryover
signatures.description: The text uses few explicit logical links, weakly carries entities between sentences, and has low semantic adjacency.

rules.why: Connective density is in the low tail, suggesting weak explicit reasoning links.
rules.fix: Add causal, contrastive, and exemplifying connectors to make the argument chain explicit.

rules.why: Arguments are not consistently sustained across adjacent sentences.
rules.fix: Carry the same actors and objects through 2-3 consecutive sentences before introducing new ones.

rules.why: Adjacent sentence semantics are weakly aligned in LSA space.
rules.fix: Reorder or rewrite sentences so each one directly elaborates the previous claim.

signatures.title: Novelty burst without logic links
signatures.description: The text introduces unusually novel lexical content while under-signaling logical relations.

rules.why: Noun novelty is in the high tail, indicating rapid concept turnover.
rules.fix: Reuse canonical nouns and reduce one-off concept labels across nearby sentences.

rules.why: Content-word novelty is also in the high tail.
rules.fix: Consolidate terminology and replace near-synonymous variants with stable terms.

rules.why: Low logical connective use makes the novelty feel ungrounded.
rules.fix: Insert explicit discourse links (because, therefore, however, for example) between claims.

signatures.title: Lexical orphan chain
signatures.description: Adjacent sentences fail to maintain noun and argument continuity while core content terms rarely recur.

rules.why: Noun concepts are weakly chained sentence to sentence.
rules.fix: Open sentences by explicitly referencing the prior sentence's main entities.

rules.why: Content lemmas recur too little to anchor topic development.
rules.fix: Repeat core lemmas when shifting from definition to implication or constraint.

rules.why: Argument carriers are not preserved across adjacent sentences.
rules.fix: Sustain the same subject-object frame across neighboring sentences before branching.
